name: the-boy-in-the-striped-pajamas
description: John Boyne's The Boy in the Striped Pajamas — a devastating novel about innocence, friendship, and prejudice at Auschwitz, told through the eyes of a nine-year-old German boy.
useCase: Understanding innocence and ignorance, friendship across divides, the banality of evil, questioning authority, boundaries that divide, the cost of prejudice.
knownRisks: The novel's historical accuracy has been criticized by some Holocaust scholars
The ending is devastating — be prepared for an emotional response
The book uses a deliberately naive narrator — younger readers may miss the horror
